title: Raja Ampat Boat Rental: A Day on the Water in 2026
html:
Exploring Raja Ampat by boat is the only way to truly experience its limestone karsts and coral-rich seas. From the bustling port of Sorong, you can charter a vessel for around €300 for a half-day trip, easily arranged through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace for renting and listing boats. The archipelago's waters are part of the Pacific Ocean, specifically the Halmahera Sea.

Where to set off in Raja Ampat

Most trips begin in Sorong, the mainland gateway, where you'll find the main ferry terminal and several fuel docks. Organised tours often depart from Waisai, the capital of Waigeo Island, while private charters may start from tiny jetties at Mansuar Island or Kri Island. For a more remote launch, consider the pier at Batanta or the anchorage at Misool Island. Each offers access to different dive sites and lagoons.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Raja Ampat?

Costs range from €50 per person for a shared speedboat tour to over €2,000 per day for a fully crewed luxury yacht. A half-day trip in a small outrigger can be as low as €200 for the boat, while a full-day catamaran for up to eight guests averages €800. For a comprehensive breakdown, you can compare verified boat listings through yacht charter in Raja Ampat on BoatHire24.

Which boat to rent

  1. Traditional Phinisi, best for multi-day liveaboard trips, sleeps 6, 12 guests, from €300 per person per night.
  2. Speedboat (Carolina style), ideal for quick island-hopping to Pianemo Lagoon, capacity 4, 8, from €150 half-day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 450), stable for families, capacity up to 10, from €600 full-day.
  4. Luxury Motor Yacht (e.g. Maiora 26m), for private charter with crew, capacity 8, 12, from €1,500 per day. See the Maiora 26m charter page for full specifications.
  5. RIB (Rigid Inflatable Boat), for dive excursions and rapid transit, capacity 6, from €250 half-day.

What is the best season to rent a boat in Raja Ampat?

The high season for boating runs from October to April, coinciding with the calm southeast monsoon. During these months the seas are generally flat and visibility for diving is excellent. The local wind, known as the 'Monsoon Timur', ensures steady conditions. One local tip:

When you are booking a day charter, always check the phase of the moon, neap tides produce the strongest currents through the seaways, making some drift dives challenging.

At a glance

  • Price range: €50, €2,000+ per person/boat
  • High season months: October to April
  • Typical trip length: half-day to week-long
  • Maximum guest capacity: 12 on most crewed yachts
  • Currency: EUR
  • Peak departure ports: Sorong, Waisai, Kri Island

Questions about boat rental in Raja Ampat

Do I need a boating licence to rent a boat in Raja Ampat?

For bareboat charters (driving yourself), an Indonesian sailing licence or an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) is typically required, but for crewed charters no licence is needed as a skipper is provided, operators on BoatHire24 will confirm exact requirements.

Can I pay in euros or local currency?

Many charter companies accept payment by bank transfer or credit card in EUR, but local transactions in Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) are more common once on the ground.

How far is it from Sorong to the main dive sites?

The distance from Sorong Harbour to the famous 'Cape Kri' dive site on Kri Island is about 30 nautical miles, usually taking 1, 1.5 hours by speedboat.

What is included in a typical crewed charter price?

Most crewed yacht rentals include the skipper, fuel, meals, and soft drinks, but check each listing for specifics on park fees and snorkelling equipment.

Getting the most out of your day on the water in 2026

Planning a private boat trip in Raja Ampat gives you the freedom to explore remote bays like Wayag Lagoon at sunrise. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Raja Ampat or list your own. If you own a vessel in the region, consider listing it on the platform, become a host and tap into a global audience of travellers for the 2026 season.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Raja Ampat and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
